free_text: Excitation functions of (p,xn) reactions on nat-Ti:
Metallic foils of high purity nat-Ti thickness of
10 mg/cm**2 were irradiated with 14.5 and 8.0 MeV
protons and 35 mg/cm**2 were irradiated with 30.2 MeV
stack was: 30.2-10.3 MeV degradation of proton energy
was calculated according to C. F. Williamson
copper foils (7.3 and 20.6 mg/cm**2) and nick
were inserted into the stack for intercomparison
The incident proton energy was defined by
operating condition of the cyclotron and measured by
analyzing magnet with an accuracy of 0.2 MeV.
uncertainty of the proton energy along the stack
includes errors in the thicknesses in the target foils
The overall uncertainties of the cross
section were estimated by summing up the errors in
- number of target nuclei and decay data.
VT+On. DATA: 563 mb-> 363 mb at 10.61 MeV
replaced. RAD-DET, REL-REF deleted and STATUS added.
VT+On. RAD-DET, REL-REF deleted and STATUS
VT+On. RAD-DET, REL-REF deleted and STATUS
